name: Defense_Mech

infobox.Systems: Attraction Pulse(Science)EffectPull target towards you 1 tile.Shield Projector(Science)EffectShield tiles from damage. (2 uses per battle.)Upgrade I+1 Use ()Increases uses per battle by 1.Upgrade II+3 Area ()Shields 3 additional tiles.
infobox.Special: Flying: Flying units can move over any terrain tile.

sections.Team: The mech is available as part of theZenith Guardsquad.
sections.Strategy:
The Defense Mech's primary use in the Zenith Guard squad is to reposition or shield friendly units and buildings from being damaged by theLaser Mech's weapon, along with moving enemies into its ally Mechs weapon range. However, Shield Projector can also be used to encase buildings and objectives near enemy spawns in shields early, using good positioning to avoid friendly fire from the Laser Mech. Using it this way, Shield Projector is particularly useful against enemies that prioritize targeting multiple units and buildings, such asBlobbers.
Unlike theGravity Mech's Grav Well, Attraction Pulse fires in a line directly and will be stopped by terrain such as mountains or buildings, also making it possible to bump and damage an enemy into the Defense Mech at close range; it is required to kill an enemy in this way to get the "Get Over Here" achievement. However, since it only starts at 2HP, consider raising its health points if looking to use the bump damage further into the timeline.
Pulling enemies is somewhat unique as a displacement effect, and can be useful effect to have when enemies place themselves in tight corners; however, Attraction Pulse is very limited at what it can resolve since it is single-target displacement only. The Defense Mech is reliant on Shield Projector to resolve multiple attacks more often. The+3 Areaupgrade helps it more and is recommended to get after the first island, as it can consistently provide more utility throughout the battle to protect more buildings.
Strong pilots to look for the Defense Mech includeHarold SchmidtorKazaaakpleth, to give the Mech an additional action when pushing enemies is more preferable than pulling.
